title: lithraea caustica var. caustica
intro: Lithraea caustica var. caustica, also known as Chilean Picramnia, is a plant species native to the Andes mountains in South America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and woody stems. The medicinal properties of Lithraea caustica var. caustica lie in its alkaloid content, particularly picramine, which has been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. Lithraea caustica var. caustica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve fever, cough, and congestion.
